title: Starting a Business — What They Don't Tell You
description: Podnikání — co se o něm obvykle neříká a co skutečně rozhoduje o úspěchu.
article_text:
STARTING A BUSINESS — WHAT THEY DON'T TELL YOU

The entrepreneurship narrative is seductive: have an idea, take a risk, work hard, become successful. Social media feeds us a constant stream of 26-year-old founders and overnight success stories. The reality, as any honest entrepreneur will tell you, is considerably messier.

Around 20% of new businesses fail in their first year. By the fifth year, roughly half have closed. These are not failures of effort or intelligence — they are often failures of timing, market conditions, or simply bad luck. The survivor bias in entrepreneurship stories is extreme: we hear endlessly about the successes and almost nothing about the failures.

What actually predicts success? Research points to a few consistent factors. Domain expertise matters: founders who understand their industry deeply tend to outperform those who don't. Resilience — the ability to absorb setbacks and keep going — is perhaps more important than any business skill. And the ability to listen: successful entrepreneurs talk about iterating based on customer feedback far more than following their original vision.

Financing is another reality that surprises many first-time founders. Bank loans are hard to secure without a trading history. Venture capital is available to very few. Most small businesses are self-funded or rely on friends and family — a situation that mixes personal relationships with financial risk in uncomfortable ways.

None of this means you shouldn't start a business. The rewards — autonomy, meaning, financial upside — can be extraordinary. But going in with clear eyes about the difficulty is not pessimism. It is the first step towards building something that lasts.

examples.en: What percentage of businesses fail in the first year?

examples.cz: We only hear success stories — failures are invisible, distorting our view of the odds.

examples.en: Name three factors that research links to business success.
examples.cz: Domain expertise, resilience, ability to listen to customer feedback.

examples.en: Why is financing difficult for first-time founders?
examples.cz: Bank loans need trading history, venture capital is rare, most rely on self-funding.

examples.en: What is the article's final message?
examples.cz: Entrepreneurship is hard — go in with clear eyes, but the rewards can be extraordinary.

tasks: 1. Describe a business idea you have — what would be the main risks?
2. Write about a business that failed — what could they have done differently?
3. Interview someone who runs their own business — write 5 questions and report their answers
